players_detected.apparel_summary: Wearing a white crop top and black leggings.
Wearing a light blue tank top with the number '2' on the back, black shorts, and white shoes.
Wearing a grey long-sleeve shirt and dark shorts.
Wearing a white t-shirt and dark shorts.

skill_indicators.signature_move_detected: Player 2 (near right) exhibits a consistent, low-to-high dink motion from the kitchen line, maintaining a balanced stance.
tactical_tendencies: favors dinks to maintain rally control
attempts speed-ups when an opening is perceived
improvement_opportunities: Far side team could improve consistency on drives to avoid unforced errors.
Near side team could work on anticipating speed-ups more effectively.

brands.location_detail: On the red wall above the court

brand_detection.unidentified_equipment_notes: All players are using generic black paddles. No visible branding on shoes or apparel.
brand_detection.unidentified_products_notes: Brown folding chairs on the sideline, yellow pickleball.

paddle_intel.notes: All paddles appear to be generic black paddles with no discernible branding.

storytelling.narrative_arc_summary: A short doubles rally featuring dinks and an attempted speed-up ends with an unforced error going long.

predicted_badges.reasoning: The rally features multiple consecutive dinks from both teams.
The rally concludes with the ball going out of bounds.
Players maintain a consistent dink rally for several shots before the error.

badge_trigger_moments: The final shot going out of bounds at 0:05.

commentary.neutral_announcer_espn: A steady dink exchange at the net, with both teams looking for an opening. The rally concludes as the ball sails just long, resulting in a point for the near side.
commentary.hype_announcer_charged: Oh, the dink game is on fire! Back and forth, back and forth, until that one just kisses the line... or not! Out of bounds, and the point is settled!
commentary.coaching_breakdown: Good job getting to the kitchen line quickly. The dinks were generally well-placed, but the far side player needs to be more patient and selective with their speed-up attempts. That last drive was too aggressive and went long, giving away the point.
commentary.social_media_caption: Dink battle ends with an out ball! 🎾
commentary.ron_burgundy_voice: By the beard of Zeus, what a display of finesse and... well, a touch of overzealousness! The dink exchange was quite the spectacle, until, alas, the ball decided to take a vacation beyond the lines. Simply marvelous, in its own way.
commentary.chuck_norris_voice: Chuck Norris once dinked a ball so hard it created a black hole. This rally, while less cosmically impactful, still saw a ball meet its destiny beyond the court's edge.
commentary.color_commentator_banter: You know, sometimes you just gotta let 'em make the mistake, and that's exactly what happened there. Patience is a virtue, especially in the kitchen!
commentary.trash_talk_friendly: Looks like someone needs to recalibrate their aim, buddy!
commentary.announcement_text_for_tts: A steady dink exchange at the net, with both teams looking for an opening. The rally concludes as the ball sails just long, resulting in a point for the near side.

daas_signals.clip_summary_one_sentence: A doubles pickleball rally featuring dinks ends with an unforced error going long.
